repo_prior_art.cite: exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt — dependency-graph CLT and completion-envelope variance result, not a uniform confidence-length converse.

published_prior_art.cite: SandovalEtAl2026 proves information-theoretic nonasymptotic CI-rate optimality for ordinary randomized experiments; TchetgenTchetgenVanderWeele2012 and RigdonHudgens2015 give finite-sample/exact partial-interference CIs, but none states this rare simultaneous all-treated/all-control clique-block confidence-length lower bound.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The claimed frontier is conditional on the balanced clique-block submodel and its stated moderate-deviation regime; it does not assert optimality for arbitrary network graphs or for the Bernstein linear term.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: A positive answer establishes necessity of the square-root r2^r/n scale only for uniformly valid intervals over mathcal M_{n,r}^{block} in r 2^r log(1/alpha)<=c_0 n.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Make the lower-bound handle explicitly fuzzy: specify two block-schedule priors (or an Assouad cube), the block-level sufficient observation, and its Hellinger/TV calculation. A deterministic shifted finite-population pair is generally singular once an informative block is observed.

Add a corollary that substitutes kappa_n=r, Vbar_n=2^(r+1), and pi_exp=2^(-r) into the supporting upper interval, then explicitly compares its square-root term with the proposed lower bound on the same block class.

Test whether the lower-bound construction extends from disjoint clique blocks to a broader nondegenerate family of overlapping-neighborhood graphs; this would separate a graph-dependence obstruction from an ordinary independent-block rare-observation reduction.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0, with the solver tasked first to formalize a fuzzy Le Cam/Assouad lower-bound route and the exact same-class upper/lower comparison.
